literal: latent impression, residual disposition, 'perfuming'; action-noun from √vās 'to perfume, to make fragrant, to scent' (causative of √vas 'to dwell', in the sense 'to cause to dwell/linger') (vās + lyuṭ + ṭāp → vāsanā), 'a perfuming' — the lingering trace left in the mind by past experience, as scent lingers in a cloth
invariant_definition: *Vāsanā* is a latent impression — the residual disposition deposited in the mind by past action and experience, which 'perfumes' subsequent cognition and inclines one toward repetition. The metaphor is olfactory: as a cloth retains the scent of what it was stored with, the mind retains the trace of what it has done and undergone, and this trace, though dormant, shapes future tendency, memory, and desire. *Vāsanā* is closely allied to *saṃskāra* (the formative impression left by an act) and, in Buddhist usage, to *anuśaya* (the latent proclivity) and the *bīja* ('seeds') stored in the *ālaya-vijñāna*; the terms differ in emphasis but converge on the problem of how past experience persists to condition the present without a continuously manifest cause. In the commentaries on the *sthitaprajña* discourse *vāsanā* enters where the persistence of conduct after knowledge must be explained: Madhva, glossing 2.55, accounts for the slight, near-aimless activity of the knower by *prārabdha-karma* together with the lingering *vāsanā* of the realized one whose ignorance has been mostly dispelled (*prārabdha-karmaṇeṣat-tirohita-brahmaṇo vāsanayā prāyo 'lpābhisandhi-pravṛttiḥ*). More broadly, *vāsanā* is what binds even after the gross causes of action are removed, and its complete extinction (*vāsanā-kṣaya*), alongside the dissolution of mind (*mano-nāśa*) and the rise of knowledge (*tattva-jñāna*), is in the yogic-Advaitic synthesis one of the marks of final liberation.

school_framing.shared_core: *Vāsanā* names **the latent residue left by past action and experience that 'perfumes' and inclines later cognition — the dormant trace by which the past conditions the present**. The traditions share the diagnostic role of such residues in sustaining tendency, desire, and the round of rebirth; they differ on their ontological seat and on whether they can finally be eradicated.
school_framing.register_axes_note: **Different ontological framing, shared functional role.** Yoga and Vedānta locate *vāsanās/saṃskāras* in the *citta* or *antaḥkaraṇa* as dispositions to be burnt up by discrimination and dissolved at liberation. Yogācāra locates the *vāsanā*-seeds in the *ālaya-vijñāna* as the very continuity of the cognitive stream. In the Gītā commentaries (notably Madhva on 2.55) *vāsanā* does light explanatory work — explaining the residual conduct of the knower under *prārabdha* — rather than carrying a full metaphysical theory.

per_school.definition: In the Dvaita tradition *vāsanā* explains why even the knower still acts at all: residual dispositions, together with *prārabdha-karma*, prompt a slight and largely unintentional activity in one whose ignorance has been all but dissolved — accounting for continued embodied conduct without implying that the knower acts from fresh desire. (No verbatim Madhva gloss on this point is available in our on-disk sources; stated as the school position rather than a quotation.)

*Vāsanās* are the deposits of past experience stored in the *citta*, continuous with the *saṃskāras* and *karmāśaya*; they ripen as birth, lifespan, and experience (YS 2.13) and, latent, condition memory and tendency (YS 4.8-9). They are attenuated by *kriyā-yoga* and burnt up as discrimination matures, the residual *saṃskāras* themselves finally dissolving in the movement toward *kaivalya*.
primary_loci: Yoga-Sūtra 4.8-9 (vāsanā and the continuity of disposition)

per_school.definition: *Vāsanā* names the perfuming-seeds (*bīja*) deposited by action and cognition in the *ālaya-vijñāna*, the store-consciousness whose maturation (*vipāka*) is the continuity of the cognitive stream. The *grāha-dvaya-vāsanā* (the latent disposition to the subject-object split) renews karmic maturation until cognition is established in *vijñapti-mātratā*; here *vāsanā* carries the full weight of explaining saṃsāric continuity.
primary_loci: Triṃśikā 18-19 (vāsanā and the renewal of vipāka)
per_school.register_tag: Φ+E; reflective reader (vāsanā as bīja in ālaya)

textual_history.summary: *Vāsanā* in its technical sense — the latent impression deposited by past experience that 'perfumes' and inclines later cognition — is squarely post-Vedic; it is a term of the classical psychologies of mind (Yoga, Yogācāra Buddhism, and Vedānta), not of the śruti. The earliest layer has no doctrine of such residues under this name. Its first systematic homes are Pātañjala Yoga, where *vāsanās/saṃskāras* stored in the *citta* ripen as birth, lifespan, and experience and condition memory and tendency (YS 2.13, 4.8–9), and Yogācāra, where the perfuming-seeds in the *ālaya-vijñāna* carry the whole weight of saṃsāric continuity. In the Vedāntic Gītā commentaries the term does lighter explanatory work — e.g. Madhva on 2.55 invokes the *vāsanā* of the realised one to explain his residual conduct. There is no honest Vedic stage to record.

stages.sanskrit: sati mūle tadvipāko jātyāyurbhogāḥ || … || tatas tadvipākānuguṇānām evābhivyaktir vāsanānām
stages.rendering: The root [of the afflictions] existing, its maturation is birth, lifespan, and experience. … From that [maturation] follows the manifestation of just those latent impressions (vāsanā) that match it.
stages.note: The earliest technical fixing in our corpus: *vāsanās*, continuous with the *saṃskāras* and the *karmāśaya* (the deposit of action), ripen as the conditions of a life and, latent, condition which dispositions later surface (YS 4.8; cf. 4.9 on their continuity across time and birth). The residue is to be attenuated by *kriyā-yoga* and burnt up as discrimination matures toward *kaivalya*.

stages.sanskrit: vāsanā / bīja in the ālaya-vijñāna (sarva-bīja ālaya-vijñāna; vipāka)
stages.rendering: the perfuming-seeds [deposited] in the store-consciousness; their maturation
stages.note: The fullest metaphysical development, attributed: in Yogācāra *vāsanā* names the seeds (*bīja*) deposited by action and cognition in the *ālaya-vijñāna*, whose maturation *is* the continuity of the cognitive stream. Cited by doctrine as a parallel; the Triṃśikā is not in our on-disk corpus. This systematic theory should not be read back into the lighter Vedāntic uses below.

stages.locus: Rāmānuja, Gītā-Bhāṣya on 3.34 (verbatim, on-disk ramanuja_gita_bhasya); cf. on 2.62–63
stages.sanskrit: prācīnavāsanājanitatadanububhūṣārūpo yo rāgo 'varjanīyo vyavasthitaḥ; tadanubhave pratihate cāvarjanīyo yo dveṣo vyavasthitaḥ
stages.rendering: The attraction (rāga) — having the form of the wish to experience [the object], produced by prior latent impressions (prācīna-vāsanā) — is fixed [in the sense] unavoidably; and when that experience is thwarted, the aversion (dveṣa) too is fixed unavoidably.
stages.note: In the Vedāntic Gītā commentaries *vāsanā* does applied explanatory work, tying the affect-cluster to the residue of the past: Rāmānuja derives even *rāga* and *dveṣa* themselves from *prācīna-vāsanā*, prior impressions, and (on 2.62–63) makes 'beginningless sin-vāsanā' (*anādi-pāpa-vāsanā*) the reason dwelling-on-objects is unavoidable. This use is thinner than the Yoga/Yogācāra metaphysics but shows *vāsanā* as the mechanism behind the whole craving chain.

translator_note: Render *vāsanā* as 'latent impression' or 'residual disposition', not 'memory' (which is *smṛti*, a manifest recollection) and not 'habit' in the thin behavioral sense. Keep the perfuming metaphor in view where it clarifies: a *vāsanā* lingers as scent lingers in a cloth, dormant yet shaping what follows. *Vāsanā* and *saṃskāra* are often interchangeable; where a distinction is drawn, *saṃskāra* tends to name the impression as deposited by a single act and *vāsanā* the standing disposition that results. In Yogācāra contexts the seed-and-store-consciousness theory should not be read back into the lighter Vedāntic uses, such as Madhva's on 2.55.
